trade.summary: Strong bilateral trade. Svizzera exports pharmaceuticals, chemicals, machinery, and financial services. Singapore exports electronics, chemicals, and serves as a hub for regional trade. An EFTA-Singapore FTA has been in place since 2003. Both are global financial and trading hubs.

diplomatic.summary: Excellent bilateral relations between two small, prosperous, neutral-leaning trade-dependent nations. Both value rules-based multilateral order. Singapore and Svizzera cooperate in WTO, financial regulation, and anti-money laundering frameworks. Both are leading global financial centers with complementary roles.

military.summary: No military alliance. Svizzera's neutrality and Singapore's non-aligned stance limit formal defense cooperation. Some exchanges on peacekeeping and humanitarian operations through UN frameworks.

financial.summary: Exceptionally strong financial ties. Singapore and Svizzera are the world's two leading private banking centers, cooperating on wealth management standards. Swiss banks have major Singapore operations; Singapore financial firms operate in Zurich. Both participate in global financial regulatory frameworks.

energy.summary: Limited direct energy trade. Svizzera and Singapore both rely on imports for energy. Some cooperation on LNG trading through commodity firms. Singapore's Temasek has invested in Swiss clean energy firms.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific partnership. ETH Zurich maintains partnerships with NStati Uniti and NTU. Joint research in biomedical sciences, AI, and materials science. Singapore hosts Swiss biotech and pharma R&D operations.
